data.title: ACG leases six Boeing 737-10 jets to Aerolíneas Argentinas
data.description:
Aviation Capital Group said Thursday it has signed lease agreements with Aerolíneas Argentinas for six Boeing (BA) 737-10 aircraft, with the first delivery scheduled for 2028.

The aircraft will support the Argentine flag carrier's fleet renewal and expansion plans, adding capacity from its Buenos Aires hubs at Aeroparque Jorge Newbery Airport and Ezeiza Airport on domestic and regional routes across South America.

The announcement was at the Farnborough International Airshow, an event that is traditionally one of the aviation industry's biggest venues for airlines, aircraft lessors and manufacturers to unveil aircraft orders and leasing agreements. Investors closely watch the show for signs of airline demand, production trends and the competitive positions of Boeing and Airbus.

The six aircraft will come from ACG's existing Boeing 737-10 orderbook. The lessor said the first aircraft is expected to be delivered in 2028.

Aerolíneas Argentinas said the aircraft form part of its 2027-2031 fleet plan. The airline said the larger-capacity narrowbody jets will help increase seats on high-demand domestic and regional routes while improving operating efficiency.

The Boeing 737-10 is the largest member of the 737 MAX family. The model has not yet entered commercial service and remains subject to certification by the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration.

ACG said it was a launch customer for the 737-10 and has delivery positions extending from an anticipated first delivery in late 2026 through 2033. The company said it is among the largest lessors of the aircraft variant.

data.title: J&J multiple myeloma drugs Tecvayli and Talvey boost cancer-free survival in late-stage trial
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) announced on Thursday that Tecvayli and Talvey, its FDA-approved treatments for the blood cancer multiple myeloma, provided greater benefit in reducing the risk of cancer progression or death as part of a combination regimen in a late-stage trial.

Based on topline data from its Phase 3 MonumenTAL-6 study, the New Jersey-based pharma giant said Tecvayli in combination with Talvey led to an 89% reduction in the risk of disease progression or death compared to a 73% reduction for Tecvayli + the anticancer agent pomalidomide.

The three-arm study was designed to evaluate Tecvayli + Talvey and Tecvayli + pomalidomide versus the investigator's choice of standard of care in adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who have undergone up to four prior lines of therapy.

The MONUMENTAL-6 study reached the primary endpoint, indicating statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvements in progression-free survival for both investigational arms and overall survival, a key secondary endpoint, according to JNJ.

The overall safety profiles for investigational arms were in line with the established safety profiles of each drug, J&J (JNJ) said, adding that given the strong results, MonumenTAL-6's independent Data Monitoring Committee recommended unblinding the study at the initial interim analysis.

data.title: IBM's outlook sees positive reactions from analysts after Q2 results
data.description:
Analysts maintained their positive views on IBM (IBM) after providing better-than-expected guidance following preliminary second quarter results last week that resulted in a stock drop of about 25%.

Shares of IBM dipped about 2% premarket on Thursday.

J.P. Morgan kept its Overweight rating and $250 price target on the shares.

"Results were relatively in-line with our revised forecast post-preannouncement, with better than expected guidance for the full year, but IBM now needs to execute to build investor confidence in the outlook. IBM’s preannouncement last week sparked greater levels of concern about the durability of the company’s mainframe and related software business as enterprises shift investment toward AI infrastructure," said analysts led by Brian Essex.

The analysts noted that commentary on the call implied confidence that 'tens of large deals,' which drove the miss for the quarter, were slipped deals rather than lost deals, with a third of those deals already closing this month. The analysts added that IBM adjusted its full-year outlook to reflect 4% to 5% constant currency growth for the year (from over 5% year-over-year previously) as budget pressure related to server, storage, and memory inflation that the analysts have consistently been hearing about year-to-date increased materially in the last few weeks of the second quarter.

"We remain Overweight rated as we think the current stock price reflects a more dire outlook many likely assumed after the preannouncement, but expect investors will need to see evidence of better execution before underwriting a meaningful recovery," said Essex and his team.

RBC Capital maintained its Outperform rating and $270 price target on the shares. 

"IBM delivered results and guidance which aligned with preliminary numbers last week as well as the narrative around a short-term capex disruption. We expected to see more conservatism applied to revenue guidance than FCF, but both numbers were above our expectations raising the bar for the 2H/26," said analyst Matthew Swanson and his team.

Jefferies kept its Buy rating and $260 price target on the stock.

"The quarter was disappointing, but the rapid recovery of roughly one-third of slipped mainframe deals and reiterated FCF guide support mgmt's view that timing, rather than demand, drove much of the shortfall. IBM’s recurring software base and intact FCF framework provide support, while delayed mainframe and ELA transactions offer a potential 3Q recovery catalyst, though we would avoid giving full credit for the maintained guide until a larger portion of the slipped activity is reflected in reported results," said analysts led by Brent Thill.

data.title: Frequency Electronics lands $18M in new aerospace and government contracts
data.description:
• Frequency Electronics (FEIM) announced on Thursday that it received multiple contracts with a combined value of approximately $18M.
  • The $18M contracts include an $11M award from a major aerospace prime contractor to supply precision frequency-generation systems for a proliferated satellite program.
  • It also includes another $7M in follow-on production contracts for space oscillators and non-space U.S. government programs.
  • CEO Tom McClelland said the $11M award is FEI's first contract with this customer and its second significant proliferated satellite contract in 2026, with the company expecting more wins in this market.
  • The company also said the follow-on production contracts should support higher-than-average corporate gross margins, helping drive higher production, revenue, and gross margin expansion over the next three years.

data.title: Union Pacific rides improved volume trend to earnings beat
data.description:
Union Pacific Corporation (UNP) traded higher in premarket action on Thursday after the rail giant delivered improved results with its second quarter earnings report.

Operating revenue rose 12% year over year to $6.9B, driven by higher fuel surcharges, volume growth, core pricing gains, and greater other revenue, partially offset by business mix. Freight revenue increased 12% during the quarter, and freight revenue excluding fuel surcharge grew 4%.

On an adjusted basis, Union Pacific's (UNP) operating ratio improved by 110 basis points to 59.2%, net income increased 12% to $2.0B, and adjusted diluted EPS was 13% higher at $3.41 to top the consensus estimate of $3.23.

"Strong execution and volume growth enabled another successful quarter and record financial results," noted Union Pacific (UNP) CEO Jim Vena. "Looking ahead, we are prepared to meet increasing customer demand with best-in-class safety, service, and operational excellence. Additionally, we are ready to move forward in the regulatory process and deliver the benefits of America's first transcontinental railroad, offering greater competition, better service, and a stronger supply chain," he added.

Shares of Union Pacific (UNP) were 1.5% higher at $296.67 in the premarket session. The 52-week high for the rail stock is $303.15.

data.title: These financial stocks are trading in overbought territory as earnings season picks up
data.description:
Financial stocks are in focus as second-quarter earnings season gains momentum, with investors assessing whether strong recent rallies across banks, insurers and fintech companies can continue.

Several names in the sector are now trading with elevated Relative Strength Index readings, signaling robust price momentum ahead of a busy slate of earnings releases and management updates.

Wilson Bank Holding Company (WBHC) tops the list with a perfect RSI of 100, reflecting exceptional recent price strength. Oicintra, Inc. (OICT) and NCR Atleos Corporation (NATL) follow with RSI readings of 78 and 77, respectively, while First BanCorp. (FBP) and The Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV) round out the top five with RSIs of 76.

Other notable names include Columbia Financial, Inc. (CLBK), Globe Life Inc. (GL), PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL), Payoneer Global Inc. (PAYO) and Cullen/Frost Bankers, Inc. (CFR), all posting RSI readings of 70 or higher.

The Relative Strength Index is a widely used momentum indicator that measures the pace and magnitude of recent price moves on a scale of 0 to 100. Readings above 70 typically suggest a stock has entered overbought territory following a sustained rally, while readings below 30 indicate oversold conditions.

Although a high RSI does not necessarily signal an imminent reversal, investors often monitor these stocks closely during earnings season, when financial results and forward guidance can either reinforce bullish momentum or spark profit-taking.

Here is the list:

  1. Wilson Bank Holding Company (WBHC), RSI: 100

  2. Oicintra, Inc. (OICT), RSI: 78

  3. NCR Atleos Corporation (NATL), RSI: 77

  4. First BanCorp. (FBP), RSI: 76

  5. The Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV), RSI: 76

  6. Columbia Financial, Inc. (CLBK), RSI: 76

  7. Globe Life Inc. (GL), RSI: 75

  8. PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL), RSI: 74

  9. Payoneer Global Inc. (PAYO), RSI: 73

  10. Cullen/Frost Bankers, Inc. (CFR), RSI: 70

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)

data.title: Syntec Optics joins Russell 3000 Index; shares drop premarket
data.description:
• Syntec Optics Holdings (OPTX) Thursday said that it has been included in the Russell 3000 Index. The final, fully validated list of index members was implemented at the opening of the U.S. equity markets on Monday, June 29, 2026.
  • The company is scheduled to receive education on the Russell indexes on July 24, 2026.
  • The stock is down 4% premarket.

data.title: Cummins updates 2027 engine launch timeline after EPA's proposed rule
data.description:
• Cummins (NYSE: CMI) announced updated plans on Thursday for its Model Year 2027 North American on-highway engine launch after the U.S. EPA proposed changes to upcoming emissions rules.
  • The company will use the proposed regulatory flexibility to gradually roll out its new HELM engine platforms.
  • The company will begin limited production of its new X10 and X15 engines in January 2027, with full production expected by Q3 and Q4 2027, respectively.
  • During the transition, the company will continue producing its current X15, X12, and L9 engines.
  • The company also reaffirmed plans to launch its next-generation B platform in January 2028, while the current B6.7 engine will remain in production through 2027.
  • CEO Jennifer Rumsey said the company's long-term product strategy remains unchanged, adding that the phased rollout is intended to improve the transition while maintaining product quality.
  • The company also noted the production timeline may change once the EPA issues its final rule.
  • On the market side, Cummins shares were trading slightly lower, down around 0.1%, near $659.01 in premarket trading.
